---
title: "Suspension Euler Parity"
---

The finite S4-S6 ladder currently tracks suspension-count and Euler-parity facts. This page is a placeholder for interactive expansion.

::: {.warning-box}
The current layer is finite suspension and Euler-parity bookkeeping. It does not claim complete continuous geometry for `S^4`, `S^5`, or `S^6`.
:::

## What To Notice

- Suspension changes finite cell counts in a controlled way.
- Euler-parity facts give a checked bridge across several dimensions.
- S4, S5, and S6 pages must keep their different future roles separate.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why can one finite suspension/Euler lemma support several dimension pages without proving all future geometry on those pages?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S4-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S4-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S5-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S5-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S6-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S6-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S6-T0003"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/S4_S6/PAPER_S456_01_GENERAL_SUSPENSION_EULER_PARITY.md">Common paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S4_S6/PAPER_S456_01_GENERAL_SUSPENSION_EULER_PARITY.md">General Suspension Euler Parity</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
